title: gaultheria sanguinolenta
intro: Gaultheria sanguinolenta, also known as red mountain wintergreen, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, valued for its medicinal properties. The plant contains methyl salicylate, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for relieving pain and reducing inflammation. Gaultheria sanguinolenta can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used to treat colds, sore throats, and digestive issues, while also providing relief from arthritis and other joint-related conditions.
